mcp_servers.description: MCP server name references required by this agent.

memory.description: Memory scope for persistent agent memory.

skills.description: Skill names to load for this agent.

CancelRequestParams.description:
Parameters for `control/cancelRequest`.

Cancels a pending server-initiated request (hook callback, approval prompt). The server should treat this as if the request was denied/failed.

request_id.description: The request_id of the pending request to cancel.

oneOf.description: Approve for this session (don't ask again for this tool).

request_id.description: The request ID of the approval being resolved.

CancelRequestParams.description:
Parameters for `control/cancelRequest`.

Cancels a pending server-initiated request (hook callback, approval prompt). The server should treat this as if the request was denied/failed.

request_id.description: The request_id of the pending request to cancel.

key.description: Specific key to read (if absent, returns all effective config).

scope.description: Config scope to write to (defaults to "user").

HookCallbackConfig.description:
SDK hook callback configuration.

Pre-registered at initialize time following the Claude Agent SDK pattern. When a hook event fires, the server emits `ServerRequest::HookCallback` with the callback_id, and the SDK client responds.

callback_id.description: Unique callback identifier (UUID, pre-registered at init).

matcher.description: Tool name pattern to match (glob-style, None = all tools).

timeout_ms.description: Timeout in milliseconds for the callback response.

error.description: Error message if the callback failed.

request_id.description: The request_id from the corresponding HookCallback.

opt_out_notification_methods.description: Notification methods the client does not want to receive.

error.description: Error message if the routing failed.

request_id.description: The request_id from the corresponding McpRouteMessage.

McpServerConfig.description: MCP server configuration for SDK clients.

oneOf.description:
SDK-managed MCP server (messages routed through SDK control channel).

The server runs in-process in the SDK client (e.g., via `@tool()` decorator). MCP messages are routed through `mcp/routeMessage` server requests.

tools.description: Tool definitions hosted by this server.

OutputFormatConfig.description: Output format configuration for structured output.

schema.description: JSON Schema for the expected output.

turn_id.description: Turn ID to rewind files to.

auto_allow_bash_if_sandboxed.description: Auto-allow Bash tool when sandboxed (avoids approval prompts).

network_access.description: Whether to allow network access in sandbox.

SdkMcpToolDef.description: Tool definition for an SDK-managed MCP server.

input_schema.description: JSON Schema for the tool's input parameters.

cursor.description: Cursor for pagination (session ID to start after).

limit.description: Maximum number of sessions to return.

prompt.description: New prompt to send in the resumed session.

hooks.description: SDK hook callbacks (pre-registered at session start).

max_budget_cents.description: Maximum budget in cents before stopping.

permission_prompt_tool.description:
MCP tool name for routing permission decisions (enterprise automation).

When set, permission approval requests are first routed to the named MCP tool. If the tool is unavailable or returns an error, the request falls back to the default SDK approval bridge.

prompt_suggestions.description: Enable prompt suggestions after each turn.

system_prompt.description: Full system prompt override or structured config.

system_prompt_suffix.description: System prompt suffix (appended to built-in system prompt).

anyOf.description: Structured system prompt with preset and optional append.

append.description: Text to append to the preset.

turn_id.description: Turn ID to interrupt (if not specified, interrupts current turn).

env.description: Environment variables to set or update.

request_id.description: The request ID of the input request being resolved.

response.description: Response payload (structure depends on the original question).

ClientRequest.description: Requests from a client to the server.

oneOf.description:
Initialize the connection (version negotiation and capabilities).

Must be the first request on a WebSocket connection. For stdio (SDK mode), this is optional — `session/start` implicitly initializes.
Start a new turn with user input.
Respond to a user input request (e.g., AskUserQuestion tool).
Change the model during a session.
Change the permission mode during a session.
Respond to an SDK hook callback.
Change thinking configuration during a session.
Rewind files to a previous turn's state.
Update environment variables during a session.
Read a session's items by ID (without resuming).
Cancel a pending server-initiated request (hook callback, approval).

ClientRequest.title: ClientRequest

key.description: Specific key to read (if absent, returns all effective config).

scope.description: Config scope to write to (defaults to "user").

oneOf.description: Allow the tool execution to proceed.
Report an error for this tool execution.

HookCallbackConfig.description:
SDK hook callback configuration.

Pre-registered at initialize time following the Claude Agent SDK pattern. When a hook event fires, the server emits `ServerRequest::HookCallback` with the callback_id, and the SDK client responds.

callback_id.description: Unique callback identifier (UUID, pre-registered at init).

matcher.description: Tool name pattern to match (glob-style, None = all tools).

timeout_ms.description: Timeout in milliseconds for the callback response.

HookCallbackConfig.title: HookCallbackConfig

oneOf.description: Allow the tool execution to proceed.
Report an error for this tool execution.

HookCallbackOutput.description: Output payload for hook callback responses.

behavior.description: What to do with the tool execution.

updated_input.description: Modified tool input (for allow behavior with changes).

HookCallbackOutput.title: HookCallbackOutput

input.description: Hook input payload (tool name, input, context).

request_id.description: Unique request identifier (for correlation with response).

error.description: Error message if the callback failed.

request_id.description: The request_id from the corresponding HookCallback.

opt_out_notification_methods.description: Notification methods the client does not want to receive.
Notification methods the client does not want to receive.

ItemStatus.description: Lifecycle status of a thread item.

oneOf.description: Tool was declined by user or permission system.

ItemStatus.title: ItemStatus

ServerCancelRequestParams.description:
Parameters for server-originated `control/cancelRequest`.

Tells the client to stop waiting for a response to the given request_id. Emitted when a hook callback times out or an approval is superseded.

request_id.description: The request_id being cancelled.

ServerCancelRequestParams.title: ServerCancelRequestParams

FileChange.description: A single file change within a `FileChangeItem`.

ItemStatus.description: Lifecycle status of a thread item.

oneOf.description: Tool was declined by user or permission system.

McpToolCallError.description: Error from a failed MCP tool call.

McpToolCallResult.description: Successful result from an MCP tool call.

content.description: Content blocks returned by the MCP server.

structured_content.description: Structured content (if provided by MCP server).

bypass_available.description:
Whether bypass-permissions mode is available for this session.

Set to `true` when the session was launched with `--dangerously-skip-permissions`. Used by the TUI to gate the ClearAndBypass option in the plan exit dialog and to enable Bypass in mode cycling.

is_error.description: Whether the agent completed with an error.

ThreadItem.description: A discrete operation within a turn.

description.description: Short description of the agent's task.

is_background.description: Whether the agent is running in background.

oneOf.description: Catch-all for other built-in tools (Read, Glob, Grep, etc.).

is_error.description: Whether the tool returned an error.

oneOf.description: Non-fatal error surfaced as an item.

Usage.description: Token usage for a turn or session.

reasoning_tokens.description: Tokens used for extended thinking / reasoning.

WorktreeExitAction.description: Whether a worktree was removed or kept on exit.

ServerNotification.description:
Events emitted by the server to all connected clients.

The `method` field uses slash-delimited paths (e.g. `"turn/started"`) following the codex-rs convention for JSON-RPC-style routing.

oneOf.description: A new session has been created.
Aggregated session result emitted before session/ended.
A new turn has started (prompt sent to model).
A retry is being attempted after a transient failure.
A new item has been created (typically in-progress).
An existing item has been updated.
An item has reached a terminal state.
A sub-agent has been moved to background execution.
Context was compacted to stay within window limits.
Context was cleared (e.g., after plan mode exit).
Model fallback started (switching to a different model).
Model fallback completed (returned to original or stabilized).
Follow-up prompt suggestions after a turn completes.
Plan mode state changed (entered or exited).
A command was queued during streaming.
A queued command was dequeued for processing.
Plugin agents have been registered and are available.
A git worktree was exited (removed or kept).
Stream stall detected (no data for extended period).
A stream request has completed with token usage.

ServerNotification.title: ServerNotification

blocked_path.description: Filesystem path that triggered a permission block (if applicable).

permission_suggestions.description: Suggested approval behaviors the SDK client may present to the user.

request_id.description: Unique request identifier (for correlation with response).

input.description: Hook input payload (tool name, input, context).

ServerRequest.description:
Requests from the server to a client.

These require a response from the client (unlike notifications which are fire-and-forget). Used for permission prompts, user input requests, and MCP message routing.

oneOf.description: Ask the client to approve a tool execution.
Ask the client for user input (e.g., AskUserQuestion tool).
Route an MCP message to a client-managed MCP server.
Cancel a previously sent request (e.g., hook callback timeout).

ServerRequest.title: ServerRequest

cursor.description: Cursor for pagination (session ID to start after).

limit.description: Maximum number of sessions to return.

turn_count.description: Number of turns in the session.

next_cursor.description: Cursor for the next page (absent if no more results).

Usage.description: Token usage for a turn or session.

reasoning_tokens.description: Tokens used for extended thinking / reasoning.

prompt.description: New prompt to send in the resumed session.
